sections.name: vamp_intro
sections.notes: Pure I chord vamp. Bass locks in, harmony comps relentlessly on shuffle. No chord changes — the groove IS the music. This establishes the feel before anything moves.

sections.name: call
sections.notes: 12-bar blues form with the Ray Charles anticipation — IV arrives a half-bar early at bar 3.5 instead of bar 4. That half-beat early hit is the signature. I chord holds long, then IV sneaks in.

sections.name: response
sections.notes: The answer phrase. Same progression but melody develops — the call got stated, now the harmony responds. Bass stays locked. Intensity rises.

sections.name: build
sections.notes: Harmonic rhythm doubles — now the IV and V chords get shorter windows, changes come faster. The anticipation is still there but compressed. This is where the crowd starts hollering back.

sections.name: peak
sections.notes: Maximum intensity. One-bar changes, full shuffle, melody screaming. V-IV-I turnaround at the end pulls it back to the top. This is the What'd I Say breakdown.

sections.name: drop
sections.notes: Sudden dynamic drop. Ray's trick — after the peak, strip it back to almost nothing. Sparse harmony, pedal bass, just the groove skeleton. Makes the return hit harder.

sections.name: return
sections.notes: Back to the 12-bar with the anticipation hit. Full density restored. The drop made this feel like a new beginning even though it's the same changes.
